title: Visiting the Great Wall of China

all.en: Climbing the Great Wall of China had been a dream of mine since I was a child.
all.zh: 攀爬中國長城是我從小以來的夢想。

all.en: Last October，I finally had the chance to visit this incredible ancient wonder.
all.zh: 去年十月，我終於有機會造訪這個令人驚嘆的古代奇蹟。

all.en: My tour group took an early bus from Beijing to avoid the worst crowds.
all.zh: 我的旅行團從北京搭一大早的巴士出發，以避開人最多的時段。

all.en: The weather was cool and clear，which made the long climb much easier.
all.zh: 天氣涼爽晴朗，這讓漫長的攀爬輕鬆許多。

all.en: From the top，I could see the wall stretching across the mountains like a giant dragon.
all.zh: 從頂端，我可以看到城牆像一條巨龍般橫越群山。

all.en: The history of the wall amazed me，as it took hundreds of years to build.
all.zh: 長城的歷史讓我驚嘆，因為它花了數百年才建造完成。

all.en: Our guide shared interesting stories about the soldiers who once protected this border.
all.zh: 我們的導遊分享了曾經守護這個邊界的士兵的有趣故事。

all.en: I took countless photographs，trying to capture the beauty and grand scale of the place.
all.zh: 我拍了無數張照片，試圖捕捉這個地方的美麗和壯闊。

all.en: Although my legs were sore the next day，I felt incredibly proud of my achievement.
all.zh: 雖然隔天我雙腿痠痛，但我為自己的成就感到無比驕傲。

all.en: I bought a small souvenir to remember this once-in-a-lifetime experience.
all.zh: 我買了一個小紀念品，以紀念這次千載難逢的經驗。

all.en: Visiting such a historical site reminded me how amazing human determination can be.
all.zh: 造訪這樣的歷史遺跡，讓我體會到人類的決心是多麼了不起。

all.en: Back at my hotel that night, I shared photos with my parents through a video call.
all.zh: 那晚回到飯店後，我透過視訊通話與父母分享照片。

all.en: My father, who had always wanted to visit the wall, became emotional looking at the images.
all.zh: 我一直想造訪長城的爸爸，看到那些照片時激動不已。

all.en: He promised that our entire family would return together within the next two years.
all.zh: 他承諾我們全家在兩年內會再一起回去一次。

all.en: I wrote detailed notes about each section of the wall I had explored that day.
all.zh: 我詳細記下了當天走過的每一段長城。

all.en: My guide later sent me a digital book about the wall's history through email.
all.zh: 我的導遊後來透過電子郵件寄了一本關於長城歷史的電子書給我。

all.en: I read it slowly during my flight home, fascinated by every chapter.
all.zh: 我在回家的航程中慢慢閱讀，對每一章都著迷不已。

all.en: When I returned to Taipei, I gave a short presentation about my trip to my classmates.
all.zh: 我回到臺北後，向同學做了一場關於這次旅行的簡短分享。

all.en: They asked many questions and several friends decided to plan their own visits.
all.zh: 他們提出許多問題，幾位朋友也決定規劃自己的長城之旅。

all.en: Two years later, our family trip happened, and we hiked a quieter section of the wall.
all.zh: 兩年後，我們的家族旅行成行，我們走了一段較少人的長城。

all.en: Sharing that incredible view with my parents made the dream complete in every way.
all.zh: 和父母一起欣賞那壯麗的景色，讓這個夢想以最完美的方式實現。

all.en: Our family trip to the Great Wall remained the highlight of that year for everyone.
all.zh: 我們家的長城之旅至今仍是那一年所有人的最愛回憶。

all.en: My father printed a panoramic photograph and placed it above the living room couch.
all.zh: 我爸爸印了一張全景照片，掛在客廳沙發上方。

all.en: My mother started a daily walking routine, inspired by the energy of climbing the wall.
all.zh: 我媽媽受到爬長城的活力啟發，開始每日散步的習慣。

all.en: Six months later, I began volunteering as a guide at a small history museum in Taipei.
all.zh: 六個月後，我開始在臺北一間小型歷史博物館擔任導覽志工。

all.en: I shared stories about ancient walls and how human creativity shaped great civilizations.
all.zh: 我分享了古代城牆的故事，以及人類創造力如何形塑偉大的文明。

all.en: My nephew became fascinated and asked me to take him to Beijing for his tenth birthday.
all.zh: 我外甥對此著迷，並要求我在他十歲生日時帶他去北京。

all.en: I promised him, and we have already started saving for the special trip together.
all.zh: 我答應了他，我們已經一起開始為這趟特別的旅程存錢。

all.en: Every year, I reread the digital book my guide had kindly sent after my first visit.
all.zh: 我每年都會重讀導遊在我第一次造訪後熱心寄來的電子書。

all.en: Each reading reveals new details about the workers, soldiers, and emperors of the past.
all.zh: 每一次閱讀都會發現關於過去工匠、士兵與帝王的新細節。

all.en: The Great Wall taught me that some dreams take patience but always reward the dreamer.
all.zh: 長城教會我，有些夢想需要耐心，但永遠會回報那位築夢的人。

all.en: My nephew's tenth birthday trip finally arrived, and we boarded our flight to Beijing together.
all.zh: 我外甥十歲生日的旅行終於成行，我們一起搭機飛往北京。

all.en: He had practiced reading the digital book every night for the past three months.
all.zh: 他在過去三個月每晚都練習閱讀那本電子書。

all.en: At the wall, his eyes widened in pure wonder as he saw the dragon-like ridges in person.
all.zh: 在長城上，當他親眼看到那如巨龍般的山脊時，雙眼睜大充滿驚奇。

all.en: He climbed with steady determination, refusing to stop even when his legs grew tired.
all.zh: 他堅定地往上爬，即使雙腿疲累也不肯停下。

all.en: Our same guide from years ago recognized me and warmly welcomed my excited little nephew.
all.zh: 多年前的同一位導遊認出了我，並熱情地歡迎我興奮的小外甥。

all.en: She told him stories that made the ancient soldiers feel alive and remarkably real.
all.zh: 她講的故事讓古代士兵彷彿活了起來，變得異常真實。

all.en: My nephew bought a small wooden dragon at the gift shop to match the souvenir I had kept.
all.zh: 我外甥在禮品店買了一隻小木龍，與我保存的紀念品成為一對。

all.en: That evening, we ate Beijing roast duck together and toasted to the dream we had shared.
all.zh: 那天晚上，我們一起吃北京烤鴨，舉杯慶祝我們共享的夢想。

all.en: Back home, he began studying Chinese history seriously, hoping to become a future historian.
all.zh: 回到家後，他開始認真研究中國歷史，希望未來成為一位歷史學家。

all.en: Watching his curiosity grow reminded me that dreams shared become dreams multiplied by love.
all.zh: 看著他的好奇心成長，提醒了我，分享的夢想會因愛而倍增。

all.en: My nephew's interest in Chinese history grew, and he enrolled in a special history program.
all.zh: 我外甥對中國歷史的興趣與日俱增，他報名了一項特別的歷史課程。

all.en: By high school, he had already published a short article in a youth history journal.
all.zh: 高中時，他已經在一本青少年歷史期刊上發表了一篇短文。

all.en: His article focused on the unsung workers who had labored to build the Great Wall.
all.zh: 他的文章聚焦於默默建造長城的無名勞工。

all.en: My old guide in Beijing wrote a kind letter praising his careful research and gentle voice.
all.zh: 我在北京的舊導遊寫了一封親切的信，稱讚他細心的研究與溫柔的筆觸。

all.en: He saved her letter inside his notebook and reread it whenever he felt discouraged.
all.zh: 他把她的信夾在筆記本中，每當感到氣餒時就重讀一次。

all.en: For his sixteenth birthday, our family took him back to walk an even quieter wall section.
all.zh: 在他十六歲生日時，我們全家帶他回去走了一段更為寧靜的長城。

all.en: He carried his original small wooden dragon, now scratched and well-loved from years of travel.
all.zh: 他帶著最初的那隻小木龍，多年旅行下來已布滿刮痕，但備受珍愛。

all.en: At university, he chose to major in East Asian history with a focus on ancient defenses.
all.zh: 上大學後，他選擇主修東亞歷史，並專注於古代防禦工事的研究。

all.en: His professors encouraged him to apply for a research exchange program in Beijing next year.
all.zh: 他的教授鼓勵他申請明年在北京的研究交換計畫。

all.en: Watching him chase his own scholarly dream, I knew the Great Wall would always live in our family.
all.zh: 看著他追逐自己的學術夢想，我知道長城將永遠活在我們家族之中。

all.en: The following year, my son successfully won the research exchange program scholarship to Beijing.
all.zh: 隔年，我兒子成功獲得了前往北京的研究交換計畫獎學金。

all.en: He spent six months walking remote sections of the Great Wall with leading Chinese historians.
all.zh: 他花了六個月與頂尖的中國歷史學家一同走訪長城偏遠的路段。

all.en: His detailed notes filled three large notebooks, illustrated with careful pencil sketches every evening.
all.zh: 他詳細的筆記填滿了三本大型筆記本，每晚都配有細心的鉛筆素描。

all.en: When he returned home, our family hosted a quiet evening where he shared his favorite discoveries.
all.zh: 他回家時，我們家舉辦了一個寧靜的夜晚，由他分享最喜愛的發現。

all.en: His Beijing professor wrote a generous letter recommending him for graduate study abroad.
all.zh: 他在北京的教授寫了一封慷慨的推薦信，推薦他出國攻讀研究所。

all.en: He chose a small but respected university in Kyoto to deepen his East Asian historical research.
all.zh: 他選擇了京都一所雖小但備受敬重的大學，深化他的東亞歷史研究。

all.en: On weekends, he visited ancient Japanese castles and compared their defensive designs to the Great Wall.
all.zh: 週末時，他造訪日本的古城，並比較其防禦設計與長城的差異。

all.en: His thesis eventually became a quiet bridge between Chinese and Japanese historical scholarship.
all.zh: 他的論文最終成為連結中日歷史學術的安靜橋樑。

all.en: He published his first academic article during the same week his younger sister entered middle school.
all.zh: 他發表第一篇學術論文的那一週，正好是他妹妹升上國中的同一週。

all.en: From a windy Great Wall afternoon, an entire scholarly life had quietly and beautifully unfolded.
all.zh: 從一個風大的長城午後，一段完整的學術人生已靜靜而美麗地展開。

all.en: After completing his graduate studies in Kyoto, my nephew accepted a teaching post at a Taipei university.
all.zh: 完成京都的研究所學業後，我外甥接下了臺北一所大學的教職。

all.en: His introductory course on East Asian fortifications became one of the most popular electives on campus.
all.zh: 他開設的東亞防禦工事入門課程，成為校園裡最受歡迎的選修課之一。

all.en: Each semester, he invited a small group of students to travel with him to the Great Wall.
all.zh: 每個學期，他都會邀請一小群學生與他一起前往長城。

all.en: His former Beijing guide, now retired, joined many of those trips as an honorary co-leader.
all.zh: 他在北京以前的導遊現已退休，以榮譽副領隊的身分加入了許多次旅行。

all.en: They walked the same ridges together, sharing decades of stories with each new wide-eyed class.
all.zh: 他們一起走在同樣的山脊上，向每一屆睜大眼睛的新班級分享數十年來的故事。

all.en: His students published a class anthology each year, dedicating it to the workers who built the wall.
all.zh: 他的學生每年出版一本班級文集，獻給建造長城的勞工。

all.en: The anthologies were donated to museums and small libraries across both Taiwan and mainland China.
all.zh: 這些文集被捐贈給臺灣和中國大陸各地的博物館與小型圖書館。

all.en: My own small souvenir from that very first trip now lived in his university office display case.
all.zh: 我第一次旅程帶回的小紀念品，如今擺在他的大學辦公室展示櫃中。

all.en: Visiting professors often paused at the case, asking about the simple wooden carving's quiet history.
all.zh: 來訪的教授常在展示櫃前駐足，詢問那件樸素木雕背後的安靜故事。

all.en: One childhood dream, climbed step by patient step, had grown into a living legacy across generations.
all.zh: 一個童年的夢想，一步步耐心攀爬，已成長為跨越世代的活生生的傳承。

all.en: My grandchildren grew up listening to my Great Wall story before every long family road trip.
all.zh: 我的孫兒們從小聽我講萬里長城的故事，每次家族長途公路旅行前都會聽一遍。

all.en: They eventually requested an entire family pilgrimage to walk the same misty section I once climbed.
all.zh: 他們最終要求一次全家朝聖之旅，去走我當年攀爬過的那段霧氣繚繞的城牆。

all.en: We packed warm jackets, sturdy shoes, and one notebook to record everything together as a clan.
all.zh: 我們打包了保暖外套、堅固鞋子，以及一本筆記本，作為家族一起記錄一切之用。

all.en: The youngest grandchild held my hand at every steep step, whispering encouragements in a soft voice.
all.zh: 最小的孫兒在每一個陡峭階梯都牽著我的手，用柔聲低語給我鼓勵。

all.en: Reaching the watchtower, we lit a tiny memorial candle for grandparents no longer with us.
all.zh: 抵達烽火台時，我們點起一根小小的紀念蠟燭，為已不在世的祖父母祈福。

all.en: The wind carried our quiet wishes across the mountains like a soft generational prayer.
all.zh: 風把我們安靜的願望吹過群山，宛如一段世代相傳的溫柔禱詞。

all.en: Back at our hotel, the children drew pictures of every section they had crossed that long day.
all.zh: 回到旅館後，孩子們把當天走過的每一段城牆都畫成圖畫。

all.en: We compiled the drawings into a heritage book that future descendants would surely inherit happily.
all.zh: 我們把這些畫整理成一本家族傳承之書，未來的後代必定會欣然繼承。

all.en: Each grandchild also pressed a leaf collected from the Wall into their personal travel journal.
all.zh: 每位孫兒也都把從城牆上撿到的一片葉子壓進他們個人的旅行日誌中。

all.en: Standing together at sunset, we knew that one childhood dream had truly grown into our family's heartbeat.
all.zh: 我們一同站在夕陽下，知道一個童年的夢想，真的成長為我們家族的心跳。

all.en: My youngest grandchild kept asking me bedtime questions about the watchtower for many evenings after we got home.
all.zh: 回家之後接連好幾個夜晚，我最小的孫兒在睡前不斷問我關於那座烽火台的問題。

all.en: I built him a small cardboard model of the watchtower so he could place it carefully on his bedside table.
all.zh: 我為他做了一個小型紙板烽火台模型，讓他可以小心地放在床頭櫃上。

all.en: He decorated the cardboard with tiny silver stars and named each one after a family member he loved most.
all.zh: 他在紙板上裝飾了小銀星，每一顆都以他最愛的家人命名。

all.en: On weekends, he invited his cousins over to climb a pillow staircase he called his very own Great Wall.
all.zh: 週末他會邀請表親來，爬一座他自稱「我的長城」的枕頭階梯。

all.en: We took photographs of every pillow climb and added them to the back pages of our heritage book.
all.zh: 我們把每次爬枕頭的過程拍下來，加進家族傳承之書的後幾頁。

all.en: His kindergarten teacher invited me to share the original Great Wall story at their family reading day.
all.zh: 他的幼稚園老師邀請我在家庭閱讀日分享原本的萬里長城故事。

all.en: I brought the cardboard model and a small bag of pressed leaves to pass quietly around the classroom.
all.zh: 我帶著紙板模型和一小袋押葉，安靜地讓教室裡的孩子們傳閱。

all.en: Each child later drew their own version of the Wall and the watchtower with crayons of every color.
all.zh: 每個孩子後來都用各色蠟筆畫出自己版本的長城和烽火台。

all.en: The teacher mailed me a thick envelope of their drawings, which I taped onto our family heritage wall.
all.zh: 老師把他們的畫作裝進一個厚信封寄給我，我把這些畫貼在我們家的家族傳承牆上。

all.en: Walking past those drawings each morning, I felt the Wall had quietly climbed inside many new young hearts.
all.zh: 每天早晨經過那些畫作，我感覺長城已悄悄爬進了許多年輕新生的心裡。

all.en: We started climbing early, while the morning air was still cool and fresh.
all.zh: 我們一早就開始攀登，那時晨間空氣依舊清涼宜人。

all.en: Some of the stone steps were so steep that I had to use my hands.
all.zh: 有些石階陡到我必須用上雙手才能爬。

all.en: Standing on a watchtower, I could see the wall snaking over endless hills.
all.zh: 站在烽火台上，我能看見長城蜿蜒越過綿延的群山。

all.en: Our guide explained how the wall once protected the country from invaders.
all.zh: 導遊解釋這道城牆過去如何保護國家抵禦入侵者。

all.en: I brought plenty of water because there were few shops along the way.
all.zh: 我帶了充足的水，因為沿途幾乎沒有商店。

all.en: The view from the top made every aching muscle completely worth it.
all.zh: 山頂上的景色，讓每一塊痠痛的肌肉都覺得值得。

all.en: A cool breeze swept across the ridge and dried the sweat on my face.
all.zh: 一陣涼風拂過山脊，吹乾了我臉上的汗水。

all.en: We took photos at the famous sign that marks the steepest section.
all.zh: 我們在標示最陡路段的著名告示牌前拍了照。

all.en: Walking down was tricky, so I held the railing the whole way.
all.zh: 下山很棘手，所以我全程都扶著欄杆。

all.en: Touching the ancient bricks, I felt connected to centuries of history.
all.zh: 觸摸著古老的磚塊，我感覺自己與數百年的歷史相連。
